ddls:
CREATE TABLE "populations" (
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population segment */,
  "population_name" TEXT /* Name of the population segment */,
  "population_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the population segment */,
  "population_category" TEXT /* Category or type of population */,
  "population_size" INTEGER /* Estimated population size of the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("population_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range for the group */,
  "age_group_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the age group */,
  "age_range_start" INTEGER /* Starting age of the range */,
  "age_range_end" INTEGER /* Ending age of the range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the marital status */,
  "status_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the marital status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_marital_stats" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic */,
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population segment */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the age group */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the marital status */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the population in the given marital status */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data for the marital statistic */,
  "data_year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "confidence_interval" REAL /* Confidence interval for the percentage */,
  "margin_of_error" REAL /* Margin of error for the percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_marital_stats_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES marital_status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_marital_stats_age_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_group_id") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_marital_stats_population_id FOREIGN KEY ("population_id") REFERENCES populations ("population_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, policymaker, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the data source */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data provided by the source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., government agency, research institution) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "source_last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the data source was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_imports" (
  "import_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data import */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "import_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was imported */,
  "data_file" TEXT /* File path to the imported data file */,
  "import_status" TEXT /* Status of the import (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the import failed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("import_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_imports_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "generation_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report file */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., demographic report, marital status report) */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
